title: Cephalaria mediterranea
intro: Cephalaria mediterranea, also known as Mediterranean hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Eastern Europe. Traditionally, Cephalaria mediterranea has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Cephalaria mediterranea is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, although its use may vary depending on regional traditions and available information.
taxonomy: Cephalaria mediterranea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephalaria, which is a group of plants commonly known as sea bluffs or silver sea holly. The genus Cephalaria is further classified within the family Caprifoliaceae, which includes other plants like honeysuckle and viburnum. Cephalaria mediterranea is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its tall stature and showy white flowers. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Caprifoliaceae family.
uses: Cephalaria mediterranea is known for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Cephalaria mediterranea is thought to have antioxidant and antispasmodic properties, which can aid in reducing muscle spasms and promoting overall well-being. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.

parts: Cephalaria mediterranea. The medicinal parts of Cephalaria mediterranea, also known as Mediterranean cephalaria, are primarily the flowers and leaves.
